name: Southern Bank And Trust Company
description_short: Regional bank serving eastern North Carolina and Virginia for 125 years, offering personal and business banking with emphasis on community relationships and local decision-making.
description_long:
Southern Bank and Trust Company was founded 125 years ago in Mount Olive, North Carolina, and has grown into a regional financial institution serving eastern North Carolina and Virginia. The bank positions itself as a community-focused alternative to larger national banks, emphasizing long-term relationships with customers and deep roots in local markets. The company originated as a neighborhood bank and maintains that foundational mission while expanding services across its regional footprint.

Southern Bank offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business banking products. For personal customers, they provide checking and savings accounts, student banking through their SouthernU program, wealth management services, and money transfer via Zelle. Business customers can access commercial banking, treasury management services, and small business lending programs through their Southern@Work division. The bank also emphasizes accessibility through their website and digital banking capabilities.

What distinguishes Southern Bank is their explicit commitment to personalized service and community involvement. Multiple customer testimonials on their website highlight the availability of bankers who understand their clients personally and provide responsive problem-solving. The bank actively markets their community involvement, including youth initiatives (Wins for Kids program) and a stated focus on supporting local families and small businesses. Their 125-year history in the same region is presented as evidence of stability and genuine commitment to community rather than transactional banking.

As a regional bank, Southern Bank serves a defined geographic market rather than offering national digital banking convenience. While they emphasize personal relationships, specific product details, fee structures, interest rates, and loan terms are not disclosed on their public website. Customers seeking comprehensive product comparisons or detailed pricing information would need to contact the bank directly. They appear well-suited for customers in their service area who prioritize relationship banking over digital-first or lowest-cost options. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Student banking program (SouthernU) with educational benefits
Money transfer via Zelle for peer-to-peer payments
Treasury management and working capital solutions
Youth and community investment programs (Wins for Kids)
Loan products for businesses and individuals
pros: 125-year history and stability as a regional community bank
Personalized banker relationships with accessible loan officers mentioned by multiple business customers
Comprehensive product suite including personal banking, business banking, treasury services, and wealth management
Explicit community investment programs including youth initiatives and local business support
Multiple service delivery channels with emphasis on accessibility and screen reader compatibility
Zelle money transfer integration for fast peer-to-peer payments
Specialized programs including student banking (SouthernU) and small business focus
cons: Limited geographic service area restricted to eastern North Carolina and Virginia only
Website lacks specific details on account fees, interest rates, minimum balances, and loan terms
No information provided about digital banking capabilities, mobile app features, or online-only account options
Positioning as community bank may mean less competitive rates compared to larger national banks or fintech competitors
Limited information on small business loan qualification requirements, SBA lending programs, or commercial credit terms

contact.address: 100 N Center St, Mount Olive, NC 28365

best_for: Small business owners in eastern North Carolina and Virginia seeking relationship-based commercial banking
Community-minded consumers who prioritize personal banker relationships over digital convenience
College students in their service area using the SouthernU student banking program with educational discounts
Business customers requiring treasury management and working capital solutions with local decision-makers
diagnosis: Southern Bank is best for small business owners and personal customers in eastern North Carolina and Virginia who value community-focused banking with accessible, responsive relationships over digital convenience or nationally competitive rates. Primary caveat: the bank's regional limitation and lack of transparent pricing information on their public website require direct contact to evaluate specific products and terms.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 100 N Center St, Mount Olive, NC 28365
meta_description: Southern Bank & Trust serves North Carolina and Virginia with personal and business banking emphasizing community relationships.
